primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Researchers, labs, and research support teams
what_the_project_does: RAG-based research workspace for exploring large corpora with transparent evidence and citations.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Demonstrates reusable AI infrastructure for evidence-grounded research discovery across NYU.
work_completed_to_date: Core retrieval, answer grounding, and citation-trace workflow are live in the workbench.
current_output_or_demo: Live research workbench with citation tracing and evidence-grounded retrieval
next_30_day_goal: Expand retrieval evaluation, support corpus-specific tuning, onboard pilot research groups, and instrument usage feedback loops.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Help identify pilot groups and agree on which adoption metrics to track.
funding_case_for_leadership: Funding would support productization, onboarding, evaluation, and wider deployment across research groups.

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Humanities researchers, archivists, and digital scholarship teams
what_the_project_does: AI-assisted environment for exploring early English books through translation, semantic indexing, and retrieval.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Shows that the same AI infrastructure can support humanities research, not only technical domains.
work_completed_to_date: Core archival retrieval and analysis workflows are live for corpus exploration.
current_output_or_demo: Live analysis studio for archival and corpus exploration
next_30_day_goal: Improve OCR and translation quality, add entity and topic exploration workflows, and validate retrieval against real humanities research questions.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Confirm preferred framing for humanities audiences and any access constraints.
funding_case_for_leadership: Funding would extend AI tooling into more disciplines and strengthen the case for shared research infrastructure.

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Research groups, labs, faculty, and institutional leadership
what_the_project_does: Platform for mapping generative AI projects, use cases, and capabilities across NYU research groups.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Gives leadership a coordinated view of distributed AI work across the university.
work_completed_to_date: Initial platform concept, project taxonomy, and preview workflow are defined.
current_output_or_demo: Private preview platform and network map of NYU GenAI activity
next_30_day_goal: Finalize the data model, add curation and search workflows, onboard initial labs, and establish an update and governance process.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Approve early-access messaging and decide which groups should be included first.
funding_case_for_leadership: Funding would support platform maturity, data stewardship, onboarding, and institutional coordination around GenAI work.
notes: High strategic value for institutional visibility

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Faculty, staff, and research support teams
what_the_project_does: Hands-on workshops that help faculty and staff use generative AI for research, teaching, and workflow automation.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Builds the adoption layer needed for AI platforms and tools to be used effectively.
work_completed_to_date: Workshop sessions, training materials, and a repeatable enablement model are active.
current_output_or_demo: Workshop and training system with institutional enablement focus
next_30_day_goal: Standardize the workshop curriculum, build reusable playbooks and templates, and capture participation and downstream workflow impact.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Decide which workshop metrics should be captured for reporting upward.
funding_case_for_leadership: Funding would scale AI literacy and turn one-off workshops into a repeatable enablement program.
notes: Support layer for broader platform adoption

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Public audiences, faculty-facing outreach, and communications stakeholders
what_the_project_does: Public-facing discovery site for curated puppet performances with direct venue and ticket links.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Shows ability to ship polished audience-facing tools in addition to internal research systems.
work_completed_to_date: Public site is live with searchable listings and direct venue and ticket routing.
next_30_day_goal: Improve content update workflows, expand filtering by venue and date, and track outbound engagement to ticket and venue links.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Decide whether this should be treated as ongoing communications infrastructure and whether engagement metrics should be tracked.
funding_case_for_leadership: Funding rationale is secondary, but it shows the work can also support outward-facing discovery and communications goals.

primary_users_or_beneficiaries: Public health researchers and policy-focused teams
what_the_project_does: Multimodal system for detecting and classifying tobacco products from retail shelf images.
why_it_matters_to_nyu: Shows applied AI capability in a domain with clear policy and health relevance.
work_completed_to_date: Core detection and classification pipeline exists in private preview with early policy-analysis use cases.
current_output_or_demo: Private computer vision system with shelf detection and classification
next_30_day_goal: Improve model accuracy, expand labeled data coverage, validate against additional retail environments, and package outputs for policy reporting.
support_needed_from_supervisor: Advise on what can be shared now and whether this should be part of the current funding narrative.
funding_case_for_leadership: Funding would support domain-specific applied AI work with direct public health relevance and broaden the portfolio of delivered systems.
notes: Not yet ready for broad public visibility
